purpose: Extend PyCard into a true single-window HyperCard-like stack editor with multiple cards, card navigation, whole-stack save/load, goto_card(name) scripting support, and direct manipulation resizing of objects via visible selection handles. No classes allowed.

design_intent.product_identity: PyCard remains a HyperCard-like single-window stack system, not a multi-window live-coding environment.
design_intent.architectural_rule: The application displays exactly one current card at a time. Card data belongs to the stack. Window/editor state belongs to the single application window.

card_navigation.single_window_rule: Navigating cards replaces the currently displayed card contents in the main editing surface.
ui_feedback: Show current card name somewhere visible in the window title or status area

rendering_model.rule: At any moment, only the current card's objects are rendered into the single canvas.
on_card_switch: destroy or remove all current card widgets from canvas
render all objects for new current card

resize_handles.appearance: small square handles drawn on canvas overlay
resize_handles.hit_target: clicking a handle starts resize mode instead of drag mode

resize_behavior.general_rule: Dragging a corner handle updates object x/y/width/height depending on handle

drag_vs_resize_priority: if mouse down hits a resize handle, start resize
otherwise if mouse down hits selected object body, start drag
otherwise select object under cursor or deselect if empty

object_creation.placement_rule: new objects are centered on click position

goto_card.behavior: switch current visible card to the first card whose name matches card_name

scripting.error_handling: script errors must not crash application

persistence.save_scope: entire stack, not just current card

required_functions: update_object_geometry(state, object_id, x, y, width, height)
implementation_notes: Card data must be kept separate from canvas/widget runtime references.
Only the current card's objects should have live tkinter widget references at any given time.
Selection overlay items should not be saved to JSON.
Resize handles should be canvas-drawn overlay elements, not child widgets.
Entry and Text resizing should update both stored geometry and visual widget size.
For tkinter Text and Entry widgets embedded on canvas, use canvas.create_window and update placement using canvas.coords.
When width/height changes, update the embedded widget dimensions appropriately.
If a widget type cannot honor pixel-perfect size directly, approximate reasonably and prioritize visible correctness over internal elegance.
success_criteria: User can create multiple cards in one stack.
User can move to next and previous cards in the single window.
Entire stack saves and loads correctly.
Button scripts can call goto_card(name) to navigate between cards.
Selected objects show visible corner handles.
User can drag corner handles to resize label, button, entry, and text objects.
Resize operation feels smooth and does not conflict with dragging.
